title: Re-execute Phase 31 Plan 02: GUID_DEVINTERFACE_DISK + PnP tree walk
description:
The gsd-executor worktree for 31-02 reported completion but commits were lost during worktree cleanup. Re-executing the gap closure plan directly on the main working tree.

Plan: .planning/phases/31-usb-cm-blocking/31-02-PLAN.md
File: dlp-agent/src/detection/usb.rs

Must add:
- GUID_DEVINTERFACE_DISK constant and registration
- CM_* API imports (CM_Get_Parent, CM_Get_Device_IDW, CM_Locate_DevNodeW)

- Unit tests for disk path parsing, pending identity, removal lookup
- All quality gates: build, test, clippy, fmt

title: UAT 31: re-register SanDisk with full 128-char serial for ReadOnly/FullAccess enforcement test
description: The ReadOnly UAT test failed because the device was registered with a truncated 20-char serial. The full serial is 0401EFEFB391D95AEEF2935943BEE8A8624A8FE336775FCF2B19F193B0829547F75300000000000000000000942732C7FF1A631891558107BC294AC8 (128 chars from InstanceId). Delete the existing truncated entry in dlp-admin-cli Device Registry, then paste the full serial when re-registering. ReadOnly should apply set_volume_readonly; FullAccess should allow writes. Description bug (setupdi returns Intel Bluetooth name for SanDisk) is cosmetic, does not affect enforcement.

title: fix: setupdi_description_for_device returns wrong device name (Bluetooth instead of SanDisk)
description: When SanDisk USB storage (VID=0781 PID=5591) arrives, the agent logs description=Intel(R) Wireless Bluetooth(R). setupdi_description_for_device matches the wrong device in the SetupDi device list. Cosmetic only — enforcement keys on VID/PID/serial. Fix by matching device path more precisely in the SetupDi enumeration.
